claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific invocation isolation with tool profile controls and boundary enforcement to prevent cross-provider model leakage and project mutation.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot and diff implementation uses git or filesystem walk to detect project mutations before and after agent execution.
evidence.why: This is the core mechanism preventing evidence agents from modifying target project files.

evidence.summary: agentToolProfile normalizes tool profile input to boundary, research, or full modes.
evidence.why: Tool profiles determine what capabilities are available to evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig prepares provider-specific AI configuration with profile-based tool settings.
evidence.why: This function bridges demand options with provider adapter configuration.

evidence.summary: Claude provider configuration clears tool restriction flags and sets agent_tool_profile based on normalized input.
evidence.why: Claude evidence mode relies on tool profile boundary instead of command-line restrictions.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot before execution and diff after execution detect mutations; violations become readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement of the boundary contract with explicit blocker propagation.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation has separate code paths for Codex, custom, and Claude providers with distinct argument structures.
evidence.why: Provider isolation prevents assumptions from one provider leaking to another.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses --cd, --sandbox, -c, and --output-last-message with optional --model.
evidence.why: Codex-specific invocation arguments differ from Claude provider.

evidence.summary: Claude provider uses -p flag with optional --model, --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, and --max-budget-usd.
evidence.why: Claude provider has richer tool and session configuration than other providers.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Codex dispatch does not inherit Claude default model or force read-only sandbox.
evidence.why: Explicit test coverage for provider isolation preventing cross-provider assumptions.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Claude dispatch clears tool restrictions and sets session persistence false.
evidence.why: Test evidence for Claude boundary configuration behavior.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Claude full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization.
evidence.why: Full semantic mode is a safety-critical escalation requiring explicit opt-in.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement is independent of evidence quality.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false.
evidence.why: Agent protocols explicitly forbid code writing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines allowed evidence scopes as project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents evidence from being misclassified.

assumptions: Provider CLI argument contracts (Claude -p, Codex exec) remain stable across versions.
Git ls-files and filesystem walk cover all project files that should be protected.
Tool profile names (boundary, research, full) map correctly to harness boundary expectations.
risks: Provider CLI breaking changes could break invocation isolation.
Non-git projects might have race conditions in boundary detection if file system changes between snapshots.
If tool profile naming drifts between provider and harness, boundary could be silently weakened.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch implements provider-specific isolation with boundary enforcement. Provider adapter has separate code paths per provider with distinct invocation arguments. Tool profiles control capabilities. Tests verify provider isolation and boundary blocking.
